1: /* 2: * 3: * Copyright 2019 http://www.hswebframework.org 4: * 5: * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); 6: * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. 7: * You may obtain a copy of the License at 8: * 9: * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 10: * 11: * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software 12: * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, 13: * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. 14: * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and 15: * limitations under the License. 16: * 17: */ 18: 19: package io.github.wujun728.activerecord.utils; 20: 21: import org.springframework.lang.Nullable; 22: 23: import java.util.HashMap; 24: import java.util.Map; 25: import java.util.function.Supplier; 26: 27: /** 28: * ThreadLocal 工具类,通过在ThreadLocal存储map信息,来实现在ThreadLocal中维护多个信息 29: *
e.g. 30: * ThreadLocalUtils.put("key",value);
31: * ThreadLocalUtils.get("key");
32: * ThreadLocalUtils.remove("key");
33: * ThreadLocalUtils.getAndRemove("key");
34: * ThreadLocalUtils.get("key",()->defaultValue);
35: * ThreadLocalUtils.clear();
36: * 37: * 38: * @since 2.0 39: */ 40: @SuppressWarnings("unchecked") 41: public class ThreadLocalUtil { 42: private static final ThreadLocal> LOCAL = ThreadLocal.withInitial(HashMap::new); 43: 44: /** 45: * @return threadLocal中的全部值 46: */ 47: public static Map getAll() { 48: return new HashMap<>(LOCAL.get()); 49: } 50: 51: /** 52: * 设置一个值到ThreadLocal 53: * 54: * @param key 键 55: * @param value 值 56: * @param 值的类型 57: * @return 被放入的值 58: * @see Map#put(Object, Object) 59: */ 60: public static T put(String key, T value) { 61: LOCAL.get().put(key, value); 62: return value; 63: } 64: 65: /** 66: * 设置一个值到ThreadLocal 67: * 68: * @param map map 69: * @return 被放入的值 70: * @see Map#putAll(Map) 71: */ 72: public static void put(Map map) { 73: LOCAL.get().putAll(map); 74: } 75: 76: /** 77: * 删除参数对应的值

code_snippet:
1: /* 2: * Copyright 2019 LINE Corporation 3: * 4: * LINE Corporation licenses this file to you under the Apache License, 5: * version 2.0 (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance 6: * with the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at: 7: * 8: * https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 9: * 10: * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software 11: * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT 12: * WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the 13: * License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations 14: * under the License. 15: */ 16: package com.linecorp.armeria.common; 17: 18: import static java.util.Objects.requireNonNull; 19: 20: import java.time.Instant; 21: import java.time.temporal.TemporalAccessor; 22: import java.util.Calendar; 23: import java.util.Date; 24: import java.util.function.Consumer; 25: 26: import com.google.common.base.Strings; 27: 28: import com.linecorp.armeria.common.annotation.Nullable; 29: import com.linecorp.armeria.internal.common.util.TemporaryThreadLocals; 30: 31: /** 32: * Immutable HTTP query parameters. 33: * 34: *

Building a new {@link QueryParams}

35: * 36: *

You can use the {@link QueryParams#of(String, String) QueryParams.of()} factory methods or 37: * the {@link QueryParamsBuilder} to build a new {@link QueryParams} from scratch:

38: * 39: * 
{@code
40:  * // Using of()
41:  * QueryParams paramsWithOf = QueryParams.of("the_string", "fourty-two",
42:  *                                           "the_number", 42);
43:  *
44:  * // Using builder()
45:  * QueryParams paramsWithBuilder =
46:  *     QueryParams.builder()
47:  *                .add("the_string", "forty-two")
48:  *                .add("the_number", 42)
49:  *                .build();
50:  *
51:  * assert paramsWithOf.equals(paramsWithBuilder);
52:  * }
53: * 54: *

Building a new {@link QueryParams} from an existing one

55: * 56: *

You can use {@link QueryParams#toBuilder()} or {@link QueryParams#withMutations(Consumer)} to build 57: * a new {@link QueryParams} derived from an existing one:

58: * 59: * 
{@code
60:  * QueryParams params = QueryParams.of("name1", "value0");
61:  *
62:  * // Using toBuilder()
63:  * QueryParams paramsWithToBuilder = params.toBuilder()
64:  *                                         .set("name1", "value1")
65:  *                                         .add("name2", "value2")
66:  *                                         .build();
67:  * // Using withMutations()
68:  * QueryParams paramsWithMutations = params.withMutations(builder -> {
69:  *     builder.set("name1", "value1");
70:  *     builder.add("name2", "value2");
71:  * });
72:  *
73:  * assert paramsWithToBuilder.equals(paramsWithMutations);
74:  *
75:  * // Note that the original parameters remain unmodified.
76:  * assert !params.equals(paramsWithToBuilder);
77:  * assert !params.equals(paramsWithMutations);
78:  * }
79: * 80: *

Specifying a non-{@link String} parameter value

81: * 82: *

Certain parameter values are better represented as a Java object, such as {@link Integer}, 83: * {@link MediaType}, {@link Instant} or {@link Date}, than as a {@link String}. Armeria's query parameters 84: * API allows you to specify a Java object of well-known type as a parameter value by converting it into 85: * an HTTP-friendly {@link String} representation:

86: * 87: *
    88: *
  • {@link Number}, {@link CharSequence} and {@link MediaType} 89: *
      90: *
    • Converted via {@code toString()}
      • 91: *
    • e.g. {@code "42"}, {@code "string"}, {@code "text/plain; charset=utf-8"}
      • 92: *
    93: *
    • 94: *
  • {@link CacheControl} and {@link ContentDisposition} 95: *
      96: *
    • Converted via {@code asHeaderValue()}
      • 97: *
    • e.g. {@code "no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate"}, 98: * {@code "form-data; name=\"fieldName\""}
      • 99: *
    100: *
    • 101: *
  • {@link Instant}, {@link TemporalAccessor}, {@link Date} and {@link Calendar} 102: *
      103: *
    • Converted into a time and date string as specified in 104: * RFC1123
      • 105: *
    • e.g. {@code Sun, 27 Nov 2016 19:37:15 UTC}
      • 106: *
    107: *
    • 108: *
  • All other types 109: *
    • Converted via {@code toString()}
    110: *
    • 111: *
112: * 113: *

Using {@link QueryParams#of(String, Object) QueryParams.of()} factory methods

114: * 115: * 
{@code
116:  * QueryParams params = QueryParams.of("the-number", 42,
117:  *                                     "the-media-type", MediaType.JSON_UTF_8,
118:  *                                     "the-date", Instant.now());
119:  * }
120: * 121: *

Using {@link QueryParamsBuilder}

122: * 123: * 
{@code
124:  * QueryParams params =
125:  *     QueryParams.builder()
126:  *                .setObject("the-number", 42)
127:  *                .setObject("the-media-type", MediaType.JSON_UTF_8)
128:  *                .setObject("the-date", Instant.now())
129:  *                .build();
130:  * }
131: * 132: *

Specifying value type explicitly

133: * 134: *

You might prefer type-safe setters for more efficiency and less ambiguity:

135: * 136: * 
{@code
